summary.intent: Fix three custom-value bugs in the combobox multi-select component flagged by Codex review on PR #669, then address a follow-up finding about the fix being too broad, and get the PR to a clean passing state.
summary.outcome: All three original Codex threads were fixed, replied to, and resolved; a second Codex pass triggered a refinement (blur must only suppress custom-text commits when an actual item click is pending, not universally); the refined fix was committed as 6b4c043d and a follow-up commit 'fix(components): distinguish combobox blur item clicks', with all CI checks passing.

repo: The combobox lives in crates/ars-components/src/selection/combobox/mod.rs and has a focused test suite runnable with `cargo test -p ars-components selection::combobox`.
Codex is invoked as a reviewer via `@codex review` PR comments and signals activity with 👀 reactions; threads are resolved via GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutations.
The repo uses llvm-cov (`cargo llvm-cov test`) for coverage reporting on the combobox module.
CI gates include clippy, formatting (cargo fmt --check), mutual exclusion check, snapshot tests, workspace check, and unit tests — earlier gates tend to pass before unit tests complete.

code.finding: Blur handling for multi-select: the fix must distinguish between blur caused by a pending item click (suppress custom-text commit) vs. genuine focus-away (must still commit custom text). A pending-item-selection marker was added to the core state to make this distinction.
Enter key handling: when input already matches the selected item label, the canonical selected item key must be preserved rather than overwritten.
Single-select empty custom input on commit: clearing the input must also clear the stale selection and the hidden input value.
A clippy simplification warning was present after the blur fix and required a patch before the full verification set was clean.

workflow: TDD approach was used: a failing RED test was written first to specify the correct blur behavior, then the implementation was added to make it GREEN.
Codex review is retriggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment after resolving all existing threads; the new pass begins almost immediately.
Polling loop used: sleep 120 + reactions + GraphQL thread query + `gh pr checks` combined into a single shell command to monitor Codex and CI state efficiently.
Co-authorship with OpenAI Codex is acknowledged in commit messages via `Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>`.

friction: The initial blur fix was too broad — suppressing all custom-text commits on blur rather than only when a pending item click was in progress — requiring a second round of fixes after Codex caught it.
The new adapter-facing helper did not exist yet when the test was first compiled, causing a compile-time RED failure before the implementation patch was applied.
Unit tests were the last CI gate to complete, causing several polling cycles of waiting while other checks were already green.
open_items: Adapter-level exposure of the pending-item-selection marker may need documentation or integration tests if other adapters beyond the current one need to use it.
Coverage report was captured to /tmp/combobox-llvm-cov.txt but not formally reviewed for regressions — coverage delta was not recorded.
